title: Building documentation with target OS and native arch looks incorrect
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

For release archives `Makefile` builds documentation targeting the selected OS, but using native arch. This doesn't look correct, because it can result with the pair, which just doesn't exist. I can't come up with the currently broken option, but in future if Intel Macs will become unsupported it will not be possible to compile docs on Linux_amd64 for Darwin_arm64, though Darwin_arm64 will be an absolutely valid option.

Currently it manifested, when I tried to build Windows-amd64 on M1 mac, but forgot to fix compilation issues for Windows-arm64.

Discovered trying to build installers for experiment in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13006

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Source code https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/Makefile#L689

2. Source code https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/Makefile#L710

**Describe the results you received:**

Didn't compile for windows-amd64 on M1 mac, when changes were missing only for windows-arm64

**Describe the results you expected:**

Compile fine for windows-amd64,  but fails for windows-arm64

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
commit: c0f9a923e2011a44c1d7ec752a9b6105905dfe95
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
Intentionally omitted
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

```
Intentionally omitted
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked [the Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)?**


Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Cross-compilation build procedure

comments.body:
As you've (no doubt already) seen, this area of the Makefile is super complex.  The core problem is that we need a binary build of podman that can run on the build-host.  This is needed to generate documentation...which is included in all the release targets :confounded: 

So I'm not surprised you've found trouble here, but unfortunately I have no idea how to (easily) fix it.  Perhaps something could be done with the documentation-building script such that it doesn't require podman, but instead some intermediate binary just for generating docs?  Similar to what we do with `test/version.go`?

I'm open to any/all suggestions.

Actually my bad. Line 689 is ok (tgz path is not suited for windows and only uses native OS). So, it will be NativeOS + NativeArch, which is naturally supported.

Issue is another case https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/Makefile#L710

First we read GOOS from https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/Makefile#L706, so this will not be Native, and will not result in a binary, which can run on this host. It should allow us to use GOARCH (read from the same input here https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/Makefile#L707) in https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/Makefile#L710 instead of NATIVE_ARCH. 

Now I think that $NATIVE_ARCH is a copy-paste like error from tgz buils. 

Replacing https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/Makefile#L710 with line below looks legit (or at least harmless) for me.  
```make
	$(MAKE) GOOS=$(GOOS) GOARCH=$(GOARCH) \
```

Runnable binary inside this docs build step https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/Makefile#L482 explicitly requesting native GOOS.

But I totally agree, that thing is really complicated.
@cevich  I will prepare a PR for some other things, so, might add the change mentioned above there and then tag you in review.
Sounds good, and thanks for taking the initiative.  I only remember enough of this area of the Makefile to scare me away from wanting to touch it ever again :laughing:
